title: Route self_evolve evaluation step through local model to break evaluator homogeneity
description: The self_evolve daily pipeline currently uses Claude to evaluate Claude-generated outputs and generate improvement proposals. This creates a correlated loop: the evaluator shares the same systematic biases, attractor basins, and blind spots as the agents it is evaluating. Add a config flag SELF_EVOLVE_EVALUATOR_MODEL that routes the evaluation/scoring step to the local MLX model (already used by the health and secret agents), introducing genuine model heterogeneity into the one pipeline most sen

title: Skill store integrity verification at load time, not just at ingest
description: The current security audit (soul_manager.audit_skill()) runs once when a skill is first ingested. After that, the skill is trusted indefinitely. Add a content hash stored at audit time, and verify it each time the skill is loaded into agent context. If the hash doesn't match, re-audit before loading.
resolution: No file changes detected after implementation attempt
last_error: No file changes detected after implementation attempt

title: Add knowledge-extraction detection to skill security audit
description: Extend soul_manager.audit_skill() to detect skills that read from Mira's internal soul/memory directories and then make network calls — a 'distillation attack' pattern where a crafted skill extracts Mira's identity, memory, and learned knowledge and exfiltrates it.

title: Inter-agent message sanitization: block lateral trust propagation via notes_inbox/notes_outbox
description: Add a prompt-injection and trust-manipulation detection pass on messages flowing through the notes_inbox/notes_outbox pipeline between agents. Currently, the skill audit (soul_manager.audit_skill()) checks imported skill files for malicious code, but there is no equivalent check on inter-agent messages. The explorer agent fetches external content (arxiv, reddit, HF feeds) and writes summaries into notes_outbox; the super agent reads those and dispatches to writer, socialmedia, and general agents

title: skill audit must be purely static — remove LLM reasoning as a safety signal
description: If soul_manager.audit_skill() uses an LLM call to reason about whether a skill is safe (e.g., asking Claude to evaluate the skill text and explain why it looks benign), that reasoning is untrustworthy by construction. The reading note's combined insight: (1) training-time trojans are invisible at inference time — the infected model will reason fluently about why the malicious skill is fine; (2) CoT is post-hoc narration, not actual decision tracing. An LLM vouching for a skill's safety IS the sc

title: Surface skill audit blocks in daily journal (incident traceability)
description: When soul_manager.audit_skill() blocks a skill, the event is logged — but if that log isn't actively surfaced, it's equivalent to invisible. The reading note's core insight is that weakened defenses don't make attacks harder, they make consequences harder to trace. Mira's equivalent gap: a blocked malicious skill attempt currently disappears into logs that no pipeline reads. Add a dedicated security incident sink (e.g., logs/security_incidents.jsonl) that the journal agent reads and includes in

title: Add scope-boundary declaration to heavy-agent dispatch
description: Before dispatching to heavy-tier agents (writer, researcher, analyst), inject a mandatory scope-declaration step: the agent must explicitly state what it will and won't attempt before executing. This is a pre-flight capability signal, not a post-hoc error.

title: Harness-level friction injection for high-impact tasks
description: Add a friction prompt layer to the task dispatch pipeline that requires high-impact agents (self-evolve, socialmedia/publish, heavy-tier tasks) to first articulate: (a) what irreversible action they are about to take, (b) what could go wrong, (c) their confidence level — before proceeding. This is not a user-confirmation gate (that was removed 2026-04-07), but an internal agent self-assessment step injected by the harness into the task prompt.

title: Log explicit selection rationale in writer pipeline project advancement
description: When the writer pipeline selects which project to advance (plan → write → review → finalize), the selection decision is currently embedded opaquely in model prompts. Add a structured `selection_rationale` log entry each time a project is chosen or skipped — recording which projects were considered, which was selected, and why. This makes the 'who gets advanced' decision auditable rather than hidden behind pipeline mechanics.

title: Skill audit: emit mechanism descriptions, not just category verdicts
description: Mira's soul_manager.audit_skill() currently flags dangerous patterns and returns a pass/fail verdict with a category label (e.g. 'suspicious network request', 'dangerous exec call'). The reading note's core insight is that category descriptions are epistemically insufficient — 'will write exploits' tells you nothing about trigger conditions, propagation paths, or what specifically makes something dangerous. The same gap exists in Mira's skill audit: if a skill is blocked, the log says WHAT categ

title: Source trust tagging for inter-agent task dispatch
description: Add a `trust_origin` field to task dispatch in the super agent and sub_agent module, distinguishing between three tiers: `user` (iPhone/Apple Notes), `internal` (another Mira agent), and `external` (content sourced from external feeds, web, or third-party APIs). Agents that process or re-dispatch external content (explorer fetching arxiv/reddit/HF, surfer browser automation) would tag their outputs with `trust_origin='external'`. Downstream agents — particularly writer and socialmedia — would ch

title: Inter-agent message sanitization: prompt injection circuit breaker
description: Add a `_content_looks_suspicious()` guard in the inter-agent message passing layer (notes_bridge or task dispatch) that scans content flowing from one agent to another for prompt injection patterns before it is forwarded. This mirrors the existing `_content_looks_like_error()` guard on Substack publishing, applied to the lateral channel between agents.

title: Security claim verification gate in publish pipeline
description: Add a pre-publish check that detects unverified security claims (zero-day, CVE, exploit, vulnerability, RCE, etc.) in article content and blocks publication unless the claims carry an explicit [verified: <source>] tag. This extends the existing content guard (_content_looks_like_error / preflight_check) with a new _content_has_unverified_security_claims() function.

title: Failure Pattern Behavioral Patch (Implicit Memory Analog)
description: Add a failure-pattern counter that, when a task type fails N times, directly mutates a config or parameter file rather than only logging the failure into text memory. This is the closest analog to implicit memory available in a retrieval-based agent: instead of writing 'I failed at X' into MEMORY.md (which requires retrieval + conscious application each run), the behavior itself changes by modifying a persistent parameter. Example: if writer agent times out on long drafts 3 times, automatically
